summary: This skill standardizes neural spike and behavior data from MATLAB files into a unified trial-based HDF5 format with consistent temporal binning and quality validation for neuroscience analysis pipelines.
strategy: Load MATLAB data and extract spike times, behavior data, and trial metadata using scipy.io
Filter trials to include only successful trials based on trial_success boolean array
For each successful trial, create uniform time bins from trial start to end using specified bin size
pitfalls: MATLAB cell array handling: Use `.flatten()` and check for empty cells when processing spike_times cell arrays, as some units may have no spikes in certain periods
Time alignment errors: Ensure trial start/end times are within the bounds of time_frame array before interpolation to avoid extrapolation artifacts
Bin edge vs center confusion: Use `bins[:-1] + bin_size/2` for bin centers when interpolating behavior data to match histogram bin assignments
key_concepts: Validate input file exists and contains required fields before processing
Handle empty spike trains gracefully by creating zero-filled arrays with correct dimensions
Catch interpolation errors when behavior data has gaps and implement forward-fill or linear interpolation fallbacks
